items.title: Adult Resuscitation
items.subtitle: Evidence basis for CPR, defibrillation, and drug timing

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ANZCOR Guideline 11.1–11.10: Adult Advanced Life Support
sources.subtitle: ARC/ANZCOR, 2021–2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ILCOR CoSTR: Adult Advanced Life Support
sources.subtitle: International Liaison Committee on Resuscitation, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ERC Guidelines 2021: Adult Advanced Life Support
sources.subtitle: Soar J et al., Resuscitation 2021;161:115–151

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: AHA 2025 Guidelines for CPR and ECC, Part 9: Adult ALS
sources.subtitle: American Heart Association, Circulation 2025

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: PARAMEDIC2 RCT: Epinephrine in Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest
sources.subtitle: Perkins GD et al., NEJM 2018;379:711–721. N=8,014; adrenaline improved ROSC and 30-day survival but not neurological recovery

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: ALPS RCT: Amiodarone, Lidocaine, or Placebo in Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest
sources.subtitle: Kudenchuk PJ et al., NEJM 2016;374:1711–1722. Amiodarone and lignocaine improved survival to hospital admission vs. placebo in witnessed VF/pVT

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Hypothermic Cardiac Arrest
items.subtitle: Evidence for field CPR decisions and rewarming

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ANZCOR Guideline 9.3.3: First Aid for Hypothermia
sources.subtitle: Australian Resuscitation Council, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ERC Guidelines 2021: Cardiac Arrest in Special Circumstances, Accidental Hypothermia
sources.subtitle: Truhlář A et al., Resuscitation 2021;161:152–219. No declaration of death until ≥32°C; withhold adrenaline below 30°C

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: Wilderness Medical Society Clinical Practice Guidelines: Accidental Hypothermia
sources.subtitle: McIntosh SE et al., 2024 update

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Accidental Hypothermia
sources.subtitle: Brown DJA et al., NEJM 2012;367:1930–1938. Swiss staging (HT I–IV) guides field decisions and hospital destination

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Post-Resuscitation Care (ROSC)
items.subtitle: Evidence for targeted temperature management and post-ROSC targets

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ANZCOR Guideline 11.7 & 11.8: Post-Resuscitation Therapy and TTM
sources.subtitle: Australian Resuscitation Council, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ILCOR CoSTR: Post-Cardiac Arrest Care
sources.subtitle: International Liaison Committee on Resuscitation, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ERC and ESICM Guidelines for Post-Resuscitation Care 2021
sources.subtitle: Nolan JP et al., Intensive Care Medicine 2021;47:369–421

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: AHA 2023 Focused Update on Targeted Temperature Management
sources.subtitle: American Heart Association. Avoid fever >37.7°C for ≥72 h; prehospital cold IV infusion: no benefit (Class III)

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: TTM2 RCT: Hypothermia versus Normothermia After Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest
sources.subtitle: Dankiewicz J et al., NEJM 2021;384:2283–2294. No survival benefit for 33°C vs. 37.5°C; shifted practice toward fever avoidance

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Traumatic Resuscitation
items.subtitle: Evidence for TXA, blood product ratios, and damage control resuscitation

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ANZCOR Guideline 11.10.1: Traumatic Arrest
sources.subtitle: Australian Resuscitation Council, 2024

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: RACS Early Management of Severe Trauma (EMST) Course Manual
sources.subtitle: Royal Australasian College of Surgeons, current edition

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ATLS Advanced Trauma Life Support
sources.subtitle: American College of Surgeons, 10th Edition, 2018

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: CRASH-2 Trial: Tranexamic Acid in Trauma with Significant Haemorrhage
sources.subtitle: CRASH-2 Collaborators, Lancet 2010;376:23–32. TXA within 3 hours reduces all-cause mortality (14.5% vs 16.0%, n=20,211)

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: PROPPR RCT: Transfusion Ratios in Severe Trauma
sources.subtitle: Holcomb JB et al., JAMA 2015;313:471–482. 1:1:1 ratio improved haemostasis and 24-hour survival

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Damage Control Resuscitation: Directly Addressing the Early Coagulopathy of Trauma
sources.subtitle: Holcomb JB et al., Journal of Trauma 2007;62(2):307–310. Original DCR framework; permissive hypotension and haemostatic resuscitation

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Traumatic Cardiac Arrest: Who Are the Survivors?
sources.subtitle: Lockey DJ et al., Annals of Emergency Medicine 2006;48(3):240–244. Informs criteria for initiating and continuing traumatic resuscitation

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

sources.label: (a)

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: RANZCOG Neonatal Resuscitation Guidance

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: 2022 International Consensus on CPR and ECC Science: Neonatal Life Support
sources.subtitle: Wyckoff MH et al., Circulation 2022;146(25). Initiate at 21% O₂; 3:1 compression-to-ventilation ratio

items.title: Anaphylaxis
items.subtitle: Evidence for IM adrenaline, dosing, and observation periods

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ASCIA Acute Management of Anaphylaxis Guidelines
sources.subtitle: Australasian Society of Clinical Immunology and Allergy, 2024

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: ANZCOR Guideline 9.2.5: First Aid for Anaphylaxis
sources.subtitle: Australian Resuscitation Council, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: World Allergy Organization Guidelines for the Assessment and Management of Anaphylaxis
sources.subtitle: Simons FER et al., World Allergy Organization Journal 2011;4:13–37

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: Anaphylaxis: A 2023 Practice Parameter Update
sources.subtitle: Lieberman P et al., Annals of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology, 2023

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: GA2LEN Anaphylaxis 2024 Consensus Report
sources.subtitle: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology, 2025. New clinical criteria; epinephrine every 5–15 min as needed

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Adrenaline for the Treatment of Anaphylaxis (Cochrane Review)
sources.subtitle: Sheikh A et al., 2008. No RCT evidence for antihistamines/corticosteroids as first-line; adrenaline remains the only evidence-based first-line drug

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Incidence of Clinically Important Biphasic Reactions in Anaphylaxis
sources.subtitle: Grunau BE et al., Annals of Emergency Medicine 2014;63:736–744. Biphasic reactions ~5%; supports minimum 4-hour observation

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Sepsis & Septic Shock
items.subtitle: Evidence for early antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, and vasopressors

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: Australian Sepsis Network / ACSQHC Sepsis Clinical Care Standard
sources.subtitle: Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Health Care, 2022

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: Surviving Sepsis Campaign International Guidelines 2021
sources.subtitle: Evans L et al., Intensive Care Medicine 2021;47:1181–1247

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: Surviving Sepsis Campaign Guidelines 2026
sources.subtitle: Society of Critical Care Medicine / ESICM, 2026

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Sepsis-3: The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock
sources.subtitle: Singer M et al., JAMA 2016;315(8):801–810. Defines sepsis; qSOFA as prehospital screening tool

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: ARISE RCT: Goal-Directed Resuscitation in Early Septic Shock
sources.subtitle: ARISE Investigators, NEJM 2014;371:1496–1506. EGDT not superior to usual care; flexible resuscitation targets supported

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: SMART RCT: Balanced Crystalloids versus Saline in Critically Ill Adults
sources.subtitle: Semler MW et al., NEJM 2018;378:829–839. Balanced crystalloids reduced major adverse kidney events vs. normal saline

items.title: Cardiogenic Shock
items.subtitle: Evidence for vasopressor choice and mechanical support

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: NHF/CSANZ Guidelines for Prevention, Detection and Management of Heart Failure in Australia
sources.subtitle: National Heart Foundation & CSANZ, 2018

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ESC Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Treatment of Acute and Chronic Heart Failure 2021
sources.subtitle: McDonagh TA et al., European Heart Journal 2021;42:3599–3726

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: 2025 ACC Expert Consensus Statement on Cardiogenic Shock
sources.subtitle: Morrow DA et al., JACC 2025. Norepinephrine as first-line vasopressor; SCAI shock staging

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: SOAP-II RCT: Dopamine versus Norepinephrine in Shock
sources.subtitle: De Backer D et al., NEJM 2010;362:779–789. Dopamine had more arrhythmias and excess mortality in cardiogenic shock subgroup

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: CULPRIT-SHOCK RCT: PCI Strategies in Cardiogenic Shock
sources.subtitle: Thiele H et al., NEJM 2017;377:2419–2432. Culprit-only PCI: lower 30-day mortality than immediate multivessel PCI

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Acute Coronary Syndrome
items.subtitle: Evidence for oxygen avoidance, aspirin, and prehospital treatment

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: NHF/CSANZ Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes
sources.subtitle: National Heart Foundation of Australia, ACS clinical guideline hub

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ESC Guidelines for the Management of Acute Coronary Syndromes 2023
sources.subtitle: Byrne RA et al., European Heart Journal 2023;44:3720–3826

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: 2025 ACC/AHA/ACEP/NAEMSP/SCAI Guideline for the Management of Patients With Acute Coronary Syndromes
sources.subtitle: Circulation 2025

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: DETO2X-AMI RCT: Oxygen Therapy in Suspected Acute Myocardial Infarction
sources.subtitle: Hofmann R et al., NEJM 2017;377:1240–1249. Routine O₂ in AMI with SpO₂ ≥90%: no mortality benefit at 1 year (n=6,629)

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: AVOID RCT: Air Versus Oxygen in ST-Segment–Elevation Myocardial Infarction
sources.subtitle: Stub D et al., Circulation 2015;131:2143–2150. Prehospital O₂ in STEMI increased myocardial injury and infarct size at 6 months

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Prehospital Aspirin and Nitroglycerin for ACS: A Systematic Review
sources.subtitle: Holmberg MJ et al., Resuscitation 2022. Prehospital aspirin associated with reduced 30-day mortality

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Acute Decompensated Heart Failure
items.subtitle: Evidence for CPAP, nitrates, and oxygen in cardiogenic pulmonary oedema

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: NHF/CSANZ Guidelines for Prevention, Detection and Management of Heart Failure in Australia
sources.subtitle: National Heart Foundation & CSANZ, 2018

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ESC Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Treatment of Acute and Chronic Heart Failure 2021
sources.subtitle: McDonagh TA et al., European Heart Journal 2021;42:3599–3726

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: 3CPO RCT: Noninvasive Ventilation in Acute Cardiogenic Pulmonary Edema
sources.subtitle: Gray A et al., NEJM 2008;359:142–151. CPAP/NIPPV reduced dyspnoea; no 7-day mortality benefit vs. standard oxygen

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: High-dose Intravenous Nitroglycerin for Severe Decompensated Heart Failure (SCAPE): Feasibility Pilot
sources.subtitle: Levy P et al., Ann Emerg Med 2007;50(2):144–152. High-dose GTN (2–7 mg/h IV) improved haemodynamics in SCAPE without significant adverse events (n=26)

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

sources.label: (a)

sources.title: REVERT RCT: Modified Valsalva Manoeuvre for Emergency Treatment of Supraventricular Tachycardias
sources.subtitle: Appelboam A et al., Lancet 2015;386:1747–1753. Modified Valsalva (supine leg raise) doubled SVT cardioversion rate (43% vs 17%)

sources.label: (a)

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: Therapeutic Guidelines: Respiratory, Asthma

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: GINA Global Strategy for Asthma Management and Prevention 2024
sources.subtitle: Global Initiative for Asthma, 2024

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: PRACTICAL RCT: Budesonide-Formoterol as Needed for Mild Asthma
sources.subtitle: Beasley R et al., NEJM 2019;380:2020–2030. As-needed ICS-formoterol superior to SABA alone; basis for ICS-formoterol reliever recommendation

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: 3Mg RCT: IV or Nebulised Magnesium Sulphate versus Standard Therapy for Severe Acute Asthma
sources.subtitle: Goodacre S et al., Lancet Respiratory Medicine 2013;1:293–300. Nebulised MgSO₄ improved lung function at 60 min in severe asthma

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: IV vs. Nebulised Beta2-Agonists for Acute Asthma (Cochrane Review)
sources.subtitle: Travers AH et al., Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2001. IV beta-agonists no better than nebulised salbutamol; nebulised route preferred prehospital

sources.subtitle: Global Initiative for Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease, 2024

sources.title: Effect of High Flow Oxygen on Mortality in COPD Patients in the Prehospital Setting
sources.subtitle: Austin MA et al., BMJ 2010;341:c5462. Titrated O₂ (SpO₂ 88–92%) reduced prehospital COPD mortality by 58%

sources.subtitle: Konstantinides SV et al., European Heart Journal 2020;41:543–603

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ACC/AHA 2026 Acute Pulmonary Embolism Guideline
sources.subtitle: JACC 2025

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Wells PE Score: Derivation of a Simple Clinical Model to Categorize Patients' Probability of Pulmonary Embolism
sources.subtitle: Wells PS et al., Thrombosis and Haemostasis 2000;83(3):416–420

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: PEITHO RCT: Fibrinolysis for Patients with Intermediate-Risk Pulmonary Embolism
sources.subtitle: Meyer G et al., NEJM 2014;370:1402–1411. Tenecteplase reduced haemodynamic decompensation but increased major bleeding

items.title: Stroke & TIA
items.subtitle: Evidence for thrombolysis timing, blood pressure targets, and EVT transport

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: Stroke Foundation Living Clinical Guidelines for Stroke Management
sources.subtitle: Stroke Foundation Australia, 2023

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: AHA/ASA Guidelines for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke
sources.subtitle: Powers WJ et al., Stroke 2019 (updated 2024). BP <185/110 pre-thrombolysis; <180/105 for 24 h post-thrombolysis

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: NINDS tPA Trial: Tissue Plasminogen Activator for Acute Ischemic Stroke
sources.subtitle: NINDS Investigators, NEJM 1995;333:1581–1587. TPA within 3 h improved functional outcomes (NNT 8); foundation of thrombolysis

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: ECASS-3: Thrombolysis 3 to 4.5 Hours After Acute Ischemic Stroke
sources.subtitle: Hacke W et al., NEJM 2008;359:1317–1329. TPA benefit extended to 4.5 h with acceptable safety

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: Effect of Treatment Delay, Age, and Stroke Severity on IV Thrombolysis: A Meta-Analysis
sources.subtitle: Emberson J et al., Lancet 2014;384:1929–1935. Every 15-min delay reduces good outcome by ~1%; basis for time-to-needle urgency

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: DAWN and DEFUSE-3 Trials: Endovascular Thrombectomy with Late-Window Imaging Selection
sources.subtitle: Nogueira RG et al. / Albers GW et al., NEJM 2017–2018. EVT benefit up to 16–24 h; supports rapid transport regardless of late presentation

items.disclaimer: References are provided for clinician awareness and continuing practice support. Always consult current editions of referenced works, local employer protocols, and applicable legislation.

items.title: Seizures
items.subtitle: Evidence for prehospital benzodiazepines and second-line agents

sources.label: (a)
sources.title: Therapeutic Guidelines: Neurology, Seizures and Status Epilepticus
sources.subtitle: eTG Complete, 2024

sources.label: (b)
sources.title: ILAE Updated Classification of Seizure Types
sources.subtitle: Fisher RS et al., Epilepsia 2017;58(4):522–530. Operational classification of focal vs. generalised seizure types; basis for current field descriptors

sources.label: (c)
sources.title: RAMPART RCT: Intramuscular versus Intravenous Therapy for Prehospital Status Epilepticus
sources.subtitle: Silbergleit R et al., NEJM 2012;366:591–600. IM midazolam equally effective as IV lorazepam, faster to administer prehospital

sources.label: (c)
